Plaintiffs Motion For An Order Compelling Defendants Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Rpds, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-25-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALEXEI KUCHINSKY OF THE KLEIN LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 1-415-693-9107 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL W. KELLY OF THE SQUIRE PARON BOGGS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-954-0375 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Keith McCracken's motion to compel further discovery responses and for monetary sanctions is granted in part. Defendants Starwood Hotels and Resorts Worldwide, Inc. and Kyo-ya Hotels & Resorts, LP are required to provide code-compliant verified further responses and produce documents no later than June 9, 2017to the discovery that is the subject of this motion but only as to employees who held the position of waiter/server at Pied Piper of Garden Court at any time during the period of August 24, 2015 to the last day of Mr. McCracken's employment at the Palace Hotel. Mr. McCracken has failed to show good cause for any broader discovery and such discovery implicates the privacy rights of third parties. This order is without prejudice to Mr. McCracken seeking discovery about other Palace Hotel employees later in the case on an enhanced showing of good cause. Mr. McCracken's request for monetary sanctions is denied because defendants' positions on this motion did not lack substantial justification. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk
